channel.title: Hermes Atlas — new projects
channel.description: Newly added community-built tools, skills, plugins, and integrations for Nous Research's Hermes Agent. Updated daily.

item.title: hermes-Custom-CLI-Themes (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Custom YAML skin pack for the Hermes Agent CLI with themed palettes, spinners, branding, and ASCII banners.

item.title: MrHermagi-tutorbot (Domain Applications)
item.description: Discord AI tutor bot built on Hermes Agent profiles, cron jobs, forum delivery, HTML lessons, and TTS audio.

item.title: hermes-multichannel-prompt-optimizer (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: Hermes Agent plugin that rewrites and scores prompts across CLI, TUI, Discord, Telegram, and other surfaces.

item.title: cronalytics (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Hermes Agent cron analytics and observability plugin for attributing scheduled-run usage and estimated cost.

item.title: agentplane-hermes-plugin (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Hermes plugin for spawning AgentPlane as an external Kanban worker lane.

item.title: herm (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Alternative Hermes TUI and dashboard built with OpenTUI.

item.title: eagle-eye (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: 5-layer intelligent skill retrieval for Hermes Agent with hard triggers, FTS5, synonyms, dense embeddings, and RRF fusion.

item.title: hermes-exabase-plugin (Memory & Context)
item.description: Exabase memory plugin for Hermes Agent.

item.title: AionUi (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: AionUi is a free, open-source cowork platform that allows AI agents to operate directly on a user's computer to automate tasks and manage files. It provides a unified interface that auto-detects and integrates various CLI agents while shipping with its own built-in agent engine for zero-configuration setup. The platform supports multi-step autonomous tasks, scheduled 24/7 automation via Cron, and remote access through WebUI and messaging apps. Users can leverage specialized assistants for genera

item.title: cc-switch (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: CC Switch is a cross-platform manager designed to centralize the administration of various AI coding agents and CLI tools. It is built using the Tauri 2 framework to provide a native desktop experience across Windows, macOS, and Linux. The tool allows users to manage multiple environments including Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent from a single interface. This streamlines the workflow for developers using a variety of LLM-based coding assistants.

item.title: open-design (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: Open Design is a local-first, open-source design and prototyping system that serves as an alternative to proprietary AI design platforms. It functions by detecting and orchestrating existing coding-agent CLIs on a user's machine to drive a skill-based design workflow. The system utilizes 137 composable skills and 150 design systems to generate artifacts like pitch decks and web layouts through a structured discovery and critique process. It supports a wide range of agents including Hermes, Claud

item.title: agent-of-empires (Multi-Agent & Orchestration)
item.description: Agent of Empires is a session manager for AI coding agents on Linux and macOS that simplifies the management of multiple parallel agent workflows. It utilizes tmux to ensure sessions persist across terminal crashes or reboots, providing a TUI and web dashboard for monitoring agent status. The tool supports isolated environments via Docker sandboxing and git worktrees to allow agents to operate on different codebase branches simultaneously. It integrates with a wide range of agents, including Her

item.title: ClawPhone (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: ClawPhone provides scripts and configuration guides for deploying agent CLIs, such as Hermes Agent, on Android devices. It utilizes Termux to run these tools natively, minimizing the use of heavy compatibility layers like proot. The project includes a streamlined installer for Google's Antigravity CLI and detailed setup notes for running OpenClaw in an isolated smartphone sandbox. This allows agents to interact with phone hardware via Termux:API and Termux:GUI.

item.title: hermes-labyrinth (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Hermes Labyrinth is a read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent that visualizes autonomous workflows as a structured map of events. It functions as a black-box recorder, reading local state to track prompts, tool calls, model transitions, and subagent activities without mutating session data. The tool provides deep diagnostic visibility through specialized views for skill overrides, scheduled cron tasks, and redacted journey reports. Users can export evidence in Markdown or JSON formats wh

item.title: youtube-skills (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: YouTube Skills is a collection of tools that enables AI agents to interact with YouTube content without relying on browser automation or local binaries. It utilizes the TranscriptAPI backend to bypass cloud IP blocks, providing agents with direct access to video transcripts, search results, and channel metadata. The project integrates with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code using the Agent Skills format. Users can perform tasks like summarizing videos, extracting playlist contents, and sear

item.title: hermes-agent-helm-chart (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: This unofficial community Helm chart facilitates the deployment of Nous Research's Hermes Agent on Kubernetes clusters. It provides a structured configuration model that enforces state-safety guardrails, such as single-replica constraints and specific update strategies, to prevent data corruption in persistent environments. The chart supports both direct workload management and an operator-ready mode for platform teams using custom resource definitions. It is optimized for multi-tenant isolation

item.title: hermes-agent-windows (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project is a Windows-native adaptation of the Hermes Agent that eliminates the need for WSL2 or Linux environments. It utilizes a PowerShell-based installer to deploy the agent directly into the Windows ecosystem while resolving platform-specific issues like process signal handling. The fork introduces a dedicated Web UI management panel for real-time chat, session history, and visual configuration of skills and API keys.

item.title: hermes-agent-rtk-caveman (Developer Tools)
item.description: This project provides a preconfigured setup for Hermes Agent that integrates Rust Token Killer (RTK) and Caveman templating to reduce token consumption during CLI operations. It works by wrapping standard terminal commands and outputting compact data structures, achieving a reported 90-99% reduction in token usage. The repository includes over 40 pre-configured skills covering finance management, GitHub workflows, research tools, and DevOps automation. Users can deploy the environment via an aut

item.title: hermes-desktop-os1 (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Desktop - OS1 Edition is a native macOS workspace designed to manage Hermes Agents running on Orgo cloud computers or SSH hosts. It provides a unified interface for VM provisioning, one-click agent installation, and real-time terminal access via websockets. The application includes built-in tools for session management, Kanban boards, and file editing with conflict checks. Additionally, it features a WebRTC-based voice mode that integrates OpenAI's Realtime API with Orgo MCP tools for voi

item.title: hermes-agent-anatomy (Guides & Docs)
item.description: This project provides a systematic technical decomposition and architectural analysis of the Hermes Agent source code. It breaks down the framework's 334,000 lines of Python code into modular guides covering the core agent loop, tool registration, and multi-provider adapters. The documentation includes visual technical illustrations and a 13-dimension comparative study against other frameworks like Claude Code and OpenClaw. Users can leverage these docs to understand complex features such as con

item.title: awesome-hermes-agent-zh (Guides & Docs)
item.description: This project is a curated Chinese-language directory of resources designed to enhance the Hermes Agent workflow. It organizes a wide array of community-developed skills, tools, and integrations based on their maturity levels. The collection provides a structured path for users to move from basic installation to advanced configurations using GUIs and cross-platform skill libraries. It specifically tracks the ecosystem of self-improving AI agents, including official Nous Research tools and third-p

item.title: Hermes-Agent-On-Android (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: Hermes-Agent-On-Android is a deployment guide and installer that enables the Hermes Agent framework to run on Android devices. It utilizes Termux and proot-distro to create a Linux environment capable of hosting the AI assistant. The project supports integration with over 200 AI models, including cloud APIs and local models via Ollama. This setup allows users to run a portable, self-evolving assistant with persistent memory and access to over 70 tools.

item.title: hermes-agent-ultra (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: Hermes Agent Ultra is a Rust-native autonomous agent runtime designed to provide a more deterministic and secure alternative to the original Hermes Agent. It implements the upstream project's functionality using a Rust-only strategy to improve execution paths, observability, and operator control. The system features a runtime policy engine for tool auditing and a session time-travel mechanism for checkpointing and replaying agent interactions. It supports a wide array of local backends including

item.title: hermes-agent-windows-R (Forks & Derivatives)
item.description: This project is a Windows-native fork of Hermes Agent designed to resolve compatibility issues with path handling, process management, and terminal encoding. It implements specific adaptations for Git Bash path conversion and introduces a multi-profile runtime system for running parallel instances. The fork includes a dedicated PowerShell-based startup workflow and a web-based Dashboard UI optimized for the Windows environment.

item.title: hermes-agent-study (Guides & Docs)
item.description: This project provides a comprehensive technical analysis and documentation series focused on the internal architecture of the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It breaks down the system's core components, including its Python-based execution loop, tool registry, and self-evolution engine, while providing nearly 100 architectural diagrams. The research also explores the project's lineage by comparing its structure to OpenClaw and investigating claims regarding its EvoMap-inspired self-improvement lo

item.title: Hermes-Agent-Action-Plan (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Agent Action Plan is a configuration framework and operational guide designed to optimize Hermes Agent deployments on Ubuntu 24. It provides a structured philosophy for setting up an autonomous assistant that prioritizes local-first tools, persistent memory, and granular system access. By implementing specific security hardening, firewall rules, and communication guidelines, the plan enables the agent to perform complex tasks while maintaining user privacy and auditability. The guide

item.title: agent-sessions (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Agent Sessions is a local-first macOS application that provides a unified interface for managing and searching history from various AI coding agents. It works by indexing local session folders and databases to allow users to inspect transcripts and browse visual outputs without uploading data to the cloud. The tool supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes, Claude, Cursor, and Codex, and features an Agent Cockpit for monitoring active iTerm2 sessions. Users can recover saved session

item.title: claude-tap (Developer Tools)
item.description: claude-tap is a local proxy and trace viewer designed to help developers inspect the internal API traffic of AI coding agents. It intercepts requests to reveal system prompts, tool schemas, and token usage by running the agent's CLI through its proxy. This allows users to debug agent behavior via request diffs and export sessions as self-contained HTML files for offline review. It supports a wide range of clients including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor CLI, and Gemini CLI.

item.title: oh-my-hermes (Developer Tools)
item.description: Oh My Hermes is an opinionated workflow layer that transforms the Hermes Agent into an autonomous software development and operations engine. It functions as a curated set of 23 skills and 6 specialized agents that manage the entire application lifecycle, from requirement clarification to production deployment. The system orchestrates external tools like Claude Code, Vercel, and Supabase while maintaining persistent memory and a Kanban-based task loop. Users interact with the agent via chat plat

item.title: hermes-agent-control-room (Multi-Agent & Orchestration)
item.description: Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents across VPS environments. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured system map, registry, and runbook library to manage agent operations. The architecture supports scaling from a single agent to specialized teams using a task bus pattern for delegation and synthesis. Users can interact with agents directly or through an optional orchestrator that serves as a central front
